evals.query: My Kora HTTP server is generated from an OpenAPI contract with an apiKey security scheme. How do I validate the API key on every request?
expected_behavior: Implements HttpServerPrincipalExtractor<Principal> and binds it with @Tag(ApiSecurity.ApiKeyAuth.class)
Declares the extractor as a default method on @KoraApp or a @Module interface that the app extends
Throws SecurityException when the key is missing or wrong, returning CompletableFuture.completedFuture(principal) otherwise
Externalizes the expected key via @ConfigSource and ${ENV} substitution rather than inlining it
Does not call a non-existent Principal.current() or request.getAttribute()

evals.query: Add Bearer JWT authentication to my Kora service that generates controllers from OpenAPI.
expected_behavior: Implements HttpServerPrincipalExtractor<Principal> tagged @Tag(ApiSecurity.BearerAuth.class)
Treats the lambda 'value' parameter as the token after the Bearer prefix and verifies it with the user's own JWT component
Builds a record implementing ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Principal from the validated claims
Notes that Kora core ships no JWT verifier, so verification uses a user-supplied @Component
Does not invent a @PreAuthorize/@Secured annotation or use field injection; uses constructor/parameter injection

evals.query: How do I enforce OAuth2 scopes on my OpenAPI-generated Kora endpoints?
expected_behavior: Returns a PrincipalWithScopes from ru.tinkoff.kora.http.common.auth so the generated transport can enforce required scopes
Binds the extractor with @Tag(ApiSecurity.OAuth.class)
Implements the scopes() method returning the token's scopes
Does not invent a Kora @Scope or @Secured annotation

evals.query: When my principal extractor throws SecurityException the client gets HTTP 500. How do I return 401/403 instead?
expected_behavior: Adds an HttpServerInterceptor that catches SecurityException in chain.process(...).exceptionally(...) and returns HttpServerResponse.of(401 or 403, ...)
Tags the interceptor @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) to apply to all controllers
Distinguishes 401 (authentication failed) from 403 (authorization failed)
Re-throws or returns HttpServerResponseException unchanged and reads its status via .code()
Does not rely on an automatic SecurityException-to-status mapping that Kora does not provide

evals.query: I have a Kora HTTP server without any OpenAPI contract. How do I require an X-API-Key header on all endpoints?
expected_behavior: Implements HttpServerInterceptor and reads the header via request.headers().getFirst("X-API-Key")
Returns HttpServerResponse.of(401, ...) early when the key is missing or invalid, otherwise calls chain.process(context, request)
Registers it globally with @Tag(HttpServerModule.class) or scopes it via @InterceptWith on a controller/route
Does not use request.getAttribute or a thread-local current-principal

evals.query: How can I turn the x-user-id header into a typed Caller object injected into my Kora controller method?
expected_behavior: Implements HttpServerRequestMapper<Caller> reading request.headers().getFirst("x-user-id")
Injects it into the @HttpRoute method with @Mapping(CallerMapper.class)
Throws to reject a missing header, relying on an error interceptor to set the status
Does not fabricate a request-attribute API or Principal.current()

evals.query: Implement HTTP Basic authentication for the apiKeyAuth-free internal endpoints of my OpenAPI Kora server.
expected_behavior: Binds HttpServerPrincipalExtractor<Principal> with @Tag(ApiSecurity.BasicAuth.class)
Base64-decodes the value after the Basic prefix and splits username:password
Validates credentials against a user-supplied service and throws SecurityException on failure
Recommends serving Basic auth over HTTPS
Does not invent a built-in Kora basic-auth filter or rely on field injection; the extractor is a graph component wired via @Tag

evals.query: What type should my principal class be in a Kora HTTP server, and where does Principal come from?
expected_behavior: States that Principal is the framework marker interface ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Principal
Defines the principal as a record/data class implementing Principal (or PrincipalWithScopes for scopes)
Clarifies there is no local Principal class to define and no Principal.current() accessor
Does not equate Principal with java.security.Principal or a third-party user-details type, and does not use field injection
